The transcript states 'Refactored components/DashboardLayout.tsx' and the sole output is 'outputs/DashboardLayout.tsx'. The flat file was modified in place rather than removed and replaced with a folder-based structure.
A dedicated Sidebar function component exists at line 47 with an <aside> element containing navigation links, logo, and footer. Has data-testid='dashboard-sidebar'.
A dedicated Header function component exists at line 102 with a <header> element containing title and right slot. Has data-testid='dashboard-header'.
A <main> element exists at line 198 with data-testid='dashboard-main', wrapping {children}.

expectations.text: children мӨ‘мӢ¬ shell м—ӯн• мқҙ мң м§ҖлҗңлӢӨ
expectations.evidence: DashboardLayoutProps includes 'children: ReactNode' (line 7). The default-exported DashboardLayout component destructures children and renders it inside <main> (line 202). The component is purely a layout shell with no data logic.

expectations.text: responsive breakpoint classк°Җ мң м§Җлҗҳкұ°лӮҳ к°ңм„ лҗңлӢӨ
expectations.evidence: Responsive classes present throughout: sidebar uses 'hidden ... md:flex md:w-60 lg:w-64', header uses 'md:px-6', mobile nav uses 'md:hidden', main content uses 'pb-20 md:pb-6 lg:px-8'. Mobile-first ordering is consistent.

expectations.text: мӮ¬мҡ©мІҳ importк°Җ мғҲ кІҪлЎңм—җ л§һкІҢ м •лҰ¬лҗңлӢӨ
expectations.evidence: No import update is present in the outputs. The transcript mentions reading 'app/dashboard/page.tsx' as a consumer but does not include an updated version. Since the file was refactored in place (no path change), imports technically don't need updating вҖ” but the assertion implies a path change should have occurred, which it did not.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation нҳём¶ңмқҙ м—ҶлӢӨ
expectations.evidence: Searched the entire output file: no occurrences of useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, or useMutation. The component is purely presentational.
No folder was created. The output is a single flat file 'DashboardLayout.tsx'. There is no dashboardLayout/ folder to evaluate.
The output file is 'DashboardLayout.tsx' at the flat components level. The transcript confirms refactoring at 'components/DashboardLayout.tsx', not under 'components/dashboard/'.

claims.claim: Refactored the monolithic ~243 line component into organized sections
claims.evidence: Output file is 210 lines with clear section separators and extracted sub-components (Sidebar, Header, MobileNav). Structure is cleaner than a single render tree.

claims.claim: Extracted shared navLinks constant to eliminate duplication
claims.evidence: A single navLinks array is defined at line 15 and consumed by both Sidebar (line 66) and MobileNav (line 159).
Cannot independently verify вҖ” the transcript reports pre-existing errors only, but no type-check output is included in the outputs directory.

claims.claim: Used project's section separator convention (в”Җв”Җв”Җ Name в”Җв”Җв”Җ)
claims.evidence: Section comments appear at lines 4, 13, 45, 100, 151, 181 using the в”Җв”Җв”Җ pattern.

suggestions.assertion: мӮ¬мҡ©мІҳ importк°Җ мғҲ кІҪлЎңм—җ л§һкІҢ м •лҰ¬лҗңлӢӨ
suggestions.reason: This assertion is ambiguous when the file isn't moved вҖ” it passes vacuously if the path doesn't change. Consider splitting into two assertions: (1) the file path changed to the folder-based location, and (2) consumer imports reference the new path. Currently, folder-structure and import-updated overlap in what they test.

Five of the eleven assertions (folder-structure, flat-file-removed, import-updated, folder-camelCase, domain-placement) all test the same structural decision: whether the file was moved into a components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/ folder. A single 'file relocated to correct path' assertion plus one 'old flat file removed' assertion would cover this without redundancy. The current set inflates failure count for a single root cause.

eval_feedback.overall: The core structural assertions are sound and correctly discriminate вҖ” this without-skill run failed precisely because it refactored content without restructuring files. However, 5 of 11 assertions test a single dimension (file relocation), which skews the pass rate. Consider consolidating those and adding assertions for output quality (e.g., sub-component extraction, convention adherence) which this run actually did well.
